headers.//: Force fresh HTML on every request. Without this Vercel's edge can serve a cached index.html for 2+ hours even after a successful deploy — caught this happening when the OTP/splash code shipped to git but old HTML stayed on the edge. s-maxage=0 disables the shared (edge) cache; max-age=0 + must-revalidate forces browsers to revalidate; no-cache covers HTTP/1.0 proxies.

rewrites.//: P2: SPA fallback. Hash fragments (#/applications) don't hit the server, so refresh on a hash route already works. But if someone types or shares a non-hash path (e.g. /privacy, /admin, /onboarding) we want to serve index.html and let JS routing pick it up — otherwise Vercel returns 404. Negative-lookahead exempts real assets (src/* files, common file extensions) so they continue to 404 properly when missing, instead of returning an HTML page that breaks downstream <script> tags.
